specific_location: Stand of Red Cedar mid-way on the north side of Bonny Reservoir between North Cove Campground and Foster Grove Campground within Bonny Reservoir State Park.
description: While birding at Bonny Reservoir, I stopped at a hedge of Red Cedar, Siberian Elm, and Eastern Cottonwoods along the north shore of the reservoir. I intended just to stay in the car and pish for a minute to see if anything was present. While pishing, an Orange-crowned and Wilsons Warbler popped out, along with a Brown Thrasher. I also noticed a bird that was seemingly larger than a warbler foraging within a Siberian Elm so I got out of the car. The bird was indeed slightly larger than most warblers, had a short, thicker bill, was stockier than a warbler, and foraged very slowly through the leaves. The crown was gray, but slightly tinged with olive. The upperparts were a grayish, olive-green. There was a dull grayish white supercilium and a darkish streak through the lores, eyes and slightly beyond the eye. There was a lightening of gray beneath the eye as well. The wings and tail were a dark grayish brown with olive edging and there was no discernible presence of wingbars. The breast and flanks were pale yellow. The throat was a whitish, pale yellow. The eyes were dark and the legs seemed bluish gray.Behaviors: Foraged slowly through treetopCall: None heardPlumage: Basic
